title: anthurium infectorium
intro: Anthurium infectorium, also known as the Poison Dart Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Arum family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant contains a toxic compound called calcium oxalate, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Anthurium infectorium include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although its toxicity requires careful handling and preparation.
